14	)
15	
16	type ReadStore interface {
17		ListProjects(ctx context.Context) ([]Project, error)
18		ListWorkflows(ctx context.Context) ([]Workflow, error)
19	}
20	
21	type Project struct {
22		ID              string         `json:"id"`
23		Name            string         `json:"name"`
24		GitHubRepo      string         `json:"github_repo"`
25		ArgoCDApp       string         `json:"argocd_app"`
26		ConfigSchemaRef string         `json:"config_schema_ref,omitempty"`
27		Metadata        map[string]any `json:"metadata"`
28		CreatedAt       time.Time      `json:"created_at"`
29		// etag carries an optional store-provided CAS token. Empty for the
30		// Postgres runtime path.
31		etag string `json:"-"`
32	}
33	
34	// ETag exposes the store-provided CAS token captured by the read that produced
35	// this Project. Empty when the current store path does not provide one.
36	func (p Project) ETag() string { return p.etag }
37	
38	// WithETag returns a copy of the project with `tag` as its captured etag.
39	// Used by store implementations and tests to attach the etag at read time.
40	func (p Project) WithETag(tag string) Project { p.etag = tag; return p }
41	
42	type Workflow struct {
43		ID                  string              `json:"id"`
44		Project             string              `json:"project"`
45		Name                string              `json:"name"`
46		SchemaRef           string              `json:"schema_ref"`
47		Phases              []PhaseSpec         `json:"phases"`
48		PR                  PrPrimitive         `json:"pr"`
49		Budget              budget.Config       `json:"budget"`
50		Constraints         WorkflowConstraints `json:"constraints,omitempty"`
51		DefaultRequirements map[string]any      `json:"default_requirements"`
52		// DispatchInputs declares the dispatch-time inputs the workflow needs.
53		// Every `${{ inputs.X }}` reference inside a phase's WorkflowRef or any
54		// job's Checkout/ExtraCheckouts refs must name a declared input. The
55		// dispatcher (dashboard, MCP, replay) reads this list to render forms
56		// and validate payloads; the registration is rejected if a template
57		// reference is not backed by a declaration.
58		DispatchInputs []DispatchInputSpec `json:"dispatch_inputs,omitempty"`
59		Metadata       map[string]any      `json:"metadata"`
60		CreatedAt      time.Time           `json:"created_at"`
61		// ControlPins is the operator-owned pin document for this workflow.
62		// Pinned control values survive any re-registration: the register path
63		// enforces them into the authored payload and reports the override.
64		// Populated from the workflows.control_pins column on row reads; never
65		// part of the content-hashed schema payload.
66		ControlPins map[string]ControlPin `json:"control_pins,omitempty"`
67		// ControlChanges and PinsEnforced are write-outcome fields populated
68		// only on register/patch/pin responses (omitted on plain reads): the
69		// control-field diff vs. the previous registration, and the pin targets
70		// whose incoming values were overridden by operator pins.
71		ControlChanges []ControlChange `json:"control_changes,omitempty"`
72		PinsEnforced   []string        `json:"pins_enforced,omitempty"`
73	}
74	
75	// ControlPin freezes one control value on a workflow. Value is the canonical
76	// JSON of the pinned control (a recycle policy object, or {"total": N} for
77	// budget). PinnedBy/PinnedAt/Reason are attribution for the pin act itself.
78	type ControlPin struct {
79		Value    json.RawMessage `json:"value"`
80		PinnedBy string          `json:"pinned_by,omitempty"`
81		PinnedAt time.Time       `json:"pinned_at,omitempty"`
82		Reason   string          `json:"reason,omitempty"`
83	}
84	
85	// ControlChange is one entry in the control-field diff between the previous
86	// and the new registration of a workflow. Action is "changed", "added",
87	// "removed", or "pin_enforced" (the incoming value was discarded in favor of
88	// the operator pin).
89	type ControlChange struct {
90		Target string          `json:"target"`
91		Action string          `json:"action"`
92		From   json.RawMessage `json:"from,omitempty"`
93		To     json.RawMessage `json:"to,omitempty"`
94	}
95	
96	// WorkflowControlEvent is one attribution-ledger entry: who performed which
97	// control-plane write on a workflow, when, moving it to which schema, with
98	// the control diff or pin detail attached.
99	type WorkflowControlEvent struct {
100		ID        int64           `json:"id"`
101		Project   string          `json:"project"`
102		Name      string          `json:"name"`
103		Action    string          `json:"action"`
104		Actor     string          `json:"actor,omitempty"`
105		SchemaRef string          `json:"schema_ref,omitempty"`
106		Detail    json.RawMessage `json:"detail,omitempty"`
107		CreatedAt time.Time       `json:"created_at"`
108	}
109	
110	// DispatchInputSpec declares one dispatch-time input on a workflow registration.
111	// Name follows the same identifier rules as run-input keys. Required=true with
112	// no Default means the caller must provide a value or the dispatch is rejected
113	// at the API boundary (no fallback in the server, no implicit "main" guess).
114	// Default is filled in by the dispatch handler when the caller omits the key.
115	type DispatchInputSpec struct {
116		Name        string `json:"name"`
117		Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
118		Required    bool   `json:"required,omitempty"`
119		Default     string `json:"default,omitempty"`
120	}
121	
122	type WorkflowConstraints struct {
123		Verification VerificationConstraints `json:"verification,omitempty"`
124	}
125	
126	type VerificationConstraints struct {
127		Shape    string `json:"shape,omitempty"`
128		MaxCases int    `json:"max_cases,omitempty"`
129	}
130	
131	type PhaseSpec struct {
132		Name             string            `json:"name"`
133		Kind             string            `json:"kind"`
134		RunOn            string            `json:"run_on"`
135		Purpose          string            `json:"purpose"`
136		WorkflowFilename string            `json:"workflow_filename"`
137		WorkflowRef      string            `json:"workflow_ref"`
138		Inputs           map[string]string `json:"inputs"`
139		Outputs          []string          `json:"outputs"`
140		Requirements     map[string]any    `json:"requirements"`
141		Verify           bool              `json:"verify"`
142		RecyclePolicy    *RecyclePolicy    `json:"recycle_policy"`
143		// Always is retired. It remains only so registration can reject stale
144		// callers with a precise error instead of silently accepting the old
145		// overloaded cleanup/review contract.
146		Always                   bool            `json:"always,omitempty"`
147		EvidenceVerificationGate bool            `json:"evidence_verification_gate"`
148		DependsOn                []string        `json:"depends_on"`
149		Jobs                     []NativeJobSpec `json:"jobs"`
150		// SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv flags a teardown phase as conditional.
151		// When the originating Issue's preserve_test_env flag is true (and
152		// thus the Run's PreserveTestEnv snapshot is true), Glimmung
153		// synthesizes a "skipped" attempt for this phase instead of
154		// launching its jobs. The workflow advances past the skipped phase
155		// like a success. Used to gate the early teardown phase so the
156		// validation environment stays alive through the touchpoint gate.
157		SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv bool `json:"skip_when_preserve_test_env,omitempty"`
158	}
159	
160	type RecyclePolicy struct {
161		MaxAttempts int      `json:"max_attempts"`
162		On          []string `json:"on"`
163		LandsAt     string   `json:"lands_at"`
164	}
165	
166	type NativeJobSpec struct {
167		ID               string               `json:"id"`
168		Name             *string              `json:"name"`
169		Primitive        string               `json:"primitive,omitempty"`
170		Image            string               `json:"image"`
171		Command          []string             `json:"command"`
172		Args             []string             `json:"args"`
173		Env              map[string]string    `json:"env"`
174		Steps            []NativeStepSpec     `json:"steps"`
175		TimeoutSeconds   *int                 `json:"timeout_seconds"`
176		Managed          bool                 `json:"managed,omitempty"`
177		Checkout         *NativeCheckoutSpec  `json:"checkout,omitempty"`
178		ExtraCheckouts   []NativeCheckoutSpec `json:"extra_checkouts,omitempty"`
179		WorkingDirectory string               `json:"working_directory,omitempty"`
180		Shell            string               `json:"shell,omitempty"`
181	}
182	
183	type NativeStepSpec struct {
184		Slug             string            `json:"slug"`
185		Title            *string           `json:"title"`
186		Type             string            `json:"type,omitempty"`
187		Run              string            `json:"run,omitempty"`
188		Agent            *AgentStepSpec    `json:"agent,omitempty"`
189		Shell            string            `json:"shell,omitempty"`
190		WorkingDirectory string            `json:"working_directory,omitempty"`
191		Env              map[string]string `json:"env,omitempty"`
192		Group            string            `json:"group,omitempty"`
193		GroupTitle       *string           `json:"group_title,omitempty"`
194		DynamicGroup     *StepDynamicGroup `json:"dynamic_group,omitempty"`
195	}
196	
197	type StepDynamicGroup struct {
198		MaxItems  int    `json:"max_items,omitempty"`
199		ItemLabel string `json:"item_label,omitempty"`
200	}

1	# Workflow Execution Contract
2	
3	This contract applies to workflow registration, schema snapshots, phase/job
4	shape, native Kubernetes job launch, managed evidence gates, callback tokens,
5	and workflow sync helpers.
6	
7	## Product Model
8	
9	A Workflow is the registered automation shape for a project. It gives agents a
10	precise lane: prepare capacity, do work, verify the result, and clean up. The
11	workflow graph should be legible before dispatch and should remain reconstructable
12	after registration changes.
13	
14	## Sources Of Truth
15	
16	- Postgres `workflows` owns logical workflow registrations and current schema
17	  pointers. Its `control_pins` column is operator-owned: only the dedicated
18	  pin/unpin endpoints write it, never registration.
19	- Postgres `workflow_control_events` owns the append-only attribution ledger
20	  for control-plane writes (register, patch, pin, unpin, delete) — schema
21	  rows are content-addressed and cannot carry who-moved-the-pointer history.
22	- Historical workflow schemas referenced by runs own projection for past
23	  cycles.
24	- `docs/workflow-shape.md` owns required phases, linear topology, job
25	  concurrency, evidence gate semantics, and path-typed identity.
26	- Native Kubernetes Jobs own execution process state while running.
27	- Native job event rows own hot execution telemetry.
28	- Workflow import/sync inputs are admin conveniences only. They are not
29	  required in consumer repositories and are never the runtime source of truth.
30	
31	## Migration Rules
32	
33	- Do not make repo workflow files the dispatch source of truth.
34	- Do not register executor kinds other than `k8s_job`. Review gates are
35	  `k8s_job` phases with `purpose: review_gate`; a review-gate phase must
36	  declare exactly one `pr_merge` primitive job and no other jobs. The
37	  `pr_merge` primitive must live inside a `purpose: review_gate` phase and
38	  nowhere else.
39	- Do not add phase fan-in, fan-out, job-level dependencies, or non-linear DAG
40	  behavior without replacing this contract.
41	- Do not allow project-owned arbitrary gate jobs to stand in for the managed
42	  evidence gate.
43	- Do not delete historical schemas still referenced by run history.
44	- Do not start a workflow-execution background reconciler (run queue,
45	  dispatch timeout, completion sweep, native Job inspection, etc.) outside
46	  the `settings.ControlPlaneLoopsEnabled` gate in `cmd/glimmung-go/main.go`.
47	  The control-plane isolation boundary belongs to the
48	  [Test Slots contract](../test-slots/contract.md); a workflow-execution
49	  reconciler that ignores it lets a hot-swapped slot binary race the prod
50	  glimmung Deployment on the same runs, Postgres rows, and `glimmung-runs`
51	  Kubernetes Jobs.
52	
53	## Live Behavior
54	
55	- Registration rejects missing entry, verify, or teardown cleanup phases.
56	- Registration rejects invalid dependencies, duplicate phases, duplicate job
57	  IDs, invalid inputs, and unsupported executor kinds before they become a
58	  runtime contract.
59	- A verification phase must declare `recycle_policy` explicitly; registration
60	  rejects a nil policy naming the phase. There is no implicit platform
61	  attempt count for silence to inherit.
62	- Every workflow write flows through one choke point that enforces operator
63	  control pins: a pinned target's incoming value is discarded in favor of the
64	  pinned value before the schema hash is computed, the override is reported
65	  on the response and ledger, and a pin whose target phase is absent from the
66	  incoming registration rejects the registration. Patches naming a pinned
67	  target are rejected with the pinner, reason, and unpin remediation. No
68	  write path may mutate the workflow payload without minting a schema and a
69	  ledger event.
70	- A `verify=true` phase is a bounded verification phase whose concrete shape is
71	  selected by the persisted workflow constraint
72	  `constraints.verification.shape`. Supported shapes are `single_job`,
73	  `bounded_case_jobs`, and `dynamic_step_group`; code validates the selected
74	  primitive, but the workflow row owns which one applies. `dynamic_step_group`
75	  declares exactly one sequential verification job with one dynamic test-case
76	  block whose `dynamic_group.max_items` is at or below 10.
77	  `bounded_case_jobs` declares ten jobs named `verify-case-01` through
78	  `verify-case-10`; every case job sets `timeout_seconds` at or below 600
79	  seconds, and unused slots complete successfully with no-op case results.
80	- Jobs inside one phase launch in parallel and complete independently.
81	- A step-scoped fail-closed abort is represented by a typed `step_aborted`
82	  native event and a durable aborted step state. A failed or aborted job whose
83	  cause is step-scoped must not project with every step succeeded or
84	  not-started.
85	- A phase/job dispatch failure before a Kubernetes Job exists is represented as
86	  a failed workflow-owned `dispatch` step. Declared workflow steps remain
87	  `not_started`; the synthetic dispatch step owns the terminal failure instead
88	  of leaving the human UI without a failed node.
89	- `touchpoint_gate` is a gated native phase name, not an executor kind:
90	  reaching the `purpose: review_gate` phase creates a durable parked `k8s_job`
91	  attempt at the human decision boundary, and approve later releases that same
92	  attempt's managed `pr_merge` job through the ordinary native event,
93	  completion, watcher, and recovery paths.
94	- Phase advancement happens only after all registered jobs in the phase reach
95	  terminal callback state.
96	- Verification phases preserve verification statuses, reasons, evidence refs,
97	  and typed evidence artifacts in the phase completion. Glimmung synthesizes
98	  the phase output `verification` so the managed evidence gate has a stable
99	  JSON verdict.
100	  Multi-job verification phases aggregate per-job verification data before
101	  synthesizing that phase output.
102	- Dynamic verification groups are runtime-expanded inside one managed job from
103	  bounded plan outputs (`test_cases_json` or `test_cases_count`, plus
104	  group-specific aliases). Expanded case steps are emitted as durable native
105	  step events with concrete slugs and group metadata; template steps must not
106	  remain the only visible execution record after expansion.
107	- Evidence verification gates are canonicalized into managed Glimmung runner
108	  jobs.
109	- Dispatch may include bounded string `inputs`. These are durable run facts,
110	  persisted on the Run as `run_inputs`, copied into every native lease's
111	  metadata, exposed to native pods as `GLIMMUNG_RUN_INPUT_*`, and preserved
112	  across recycle attempts. Native `checkout.ref` and `extra_checkouts[].ref`
113	  may use exact run-input templates such as `${{ inputs.git_ref }}`; the
114	  launcher resolves them before emitting the native runner job spec so the
115	  runner receives a concrete ref.
116	- Runs use the workflow schema snapshot captured at run/cycle creation, not a
117	  later logical workflow update.
118	
119	## Failure And Recovery
120	
121	- A failed native Job produces durable job/phase failure state through the
122	  completion callback path, not a retired failure route.
123	- Teardown phases (`purpose: teardown`) are verdict-neutral. A teardown job's
124	  outcome never sets the run verdict: a failed teardown must not abort an
125	  otherwise-passing run, and must not override or mask the primary phase's
126	  verdict. The decision engine advances the cleanup chain regardless of a
127	  teardown conclusion, and terminal-cause attribution skips teardown attempts.
128	  The failed teardown remains visible on its own job/step state. Teardown jobs
129	  carry a bounded `backoffLimit` so a transient pod-start failure self-heals;
130	  producer/verify jobs keep `backoffLimit=0` (fail fast).
131	- A failed dispatch produces durable phase/job/step failure state through run
132	  terminal finalization and run graph projection, including historical rows
133	  whose child jobs or steps were previously stamped `skipped`.
134	- Callback-token validation failure must not mutate unrelated runs or phases.
135	- Workflow update failure should leave the previous logical workflow pointer
136	  intact.
137	- Service restart must preserve the ability to project active and historical
138	  cycles from schema refs and run ledgers.
139	
140	## Observability
141	
142	- Native event streams should identify project, issue, run, cycle, phase, job,
143	  step, conclusion, and relevant log tail or archive link.
144	- A pod that dies for a pod-level reason (OOMKilled, Evicted) must surface that
145	  reason, not just the Job condition. The Kubernetes Job condition collapses to
146	  `BackoffLimitExceeded` for any `backoffLimit=0` pod failure, so Glimmung reads
147	  the pod's `containerStatuses[].state.terminated.reason` / `status.reason` on
148	  terminal failure and refines the terminal reason to `oom_killed` / `evicted`
149	  (and keeps the raw reason in the human summary). This applies to outer Jobs
150	  and inner per-case Jobs alike.

output.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive Surface Map: Job-Level `when` Skip-Conditions in Native k8s_job Workflows

---

### 1. **Workflow Registration Schema Structs**

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/read_api.go:131-181`
**Job Struct:** `NativeJobSpec` (lines 166-181)

**Current Fields:**
- `ID string` — job identifier
- `Name *string` — optional display name
- `Primitive string` — managed executor primitive (empty, `pr_touchpoint`, `pr_merge`)
- `Image string` — container image
- `Command`, `Args []string` — entrypoint
- `Env map[string]string` — environment
- `Steps []NativeStepSpec` — step list
- `TimeoutSeconds *int` — job timeout
- `Managed bool` — whether runner-managed
- `Checkout, ExtraCheckouts` — git checkouts
- `WorkingDirectory, Shell string` — execution context

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/read_api.go:131-158`
**Phase Struct:** `PhaseSpec` (lines 131-158)

**Current Fields:**
- `Name, Kind, RunOn, Purpose` — phase identity
- `WorkflowFilename, WorkflowRef` — workflow reference
- `Inputs map[string]string`, `Outputs []string` — cross-phase refs
- `Requirements map[string]any` — resource requirements
- `Verify bool`, `RecyclePolicy *RecyclePolicy` — verification config
- `EvidenceVerificationGate bool` — evidence gate flag
- `DependsOn []string` — phase dependency
- `Jobs []NativeJobSpec` — jobs array
- `SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv bool` — conditional skip (lines 150-157)

**Required Additions:**
- `NativeJobSpec.When string` — job-level skip condition expression
- `PhaseSpec.Vars map[string]string` — workflow-level variables for condition evaluation

---

### 2. **Native Launcher Path: Job Iteration & Conditional Creation**

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/native_launcher.go:187-239`

**Key Function:** `LaunchNativePhase()` (lines 187-239)
- **Job iteration loop:** line 220 `for _, job := range req.Phase.Jobs`
- **Current behavior:** iterates all jobs, creates manifest for each at line 233
- **Where condition check goes:** Before line 233 `l.createJob()`
  - Evaluate `job.When` against phase context
  - If condition is false, skip manifest creation entirely
  - Job would not appear in `launched []string` return

**Job Registration Logic:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/native_launch_context.go:20-44`
- `recordLaunchedNativeJobs()` (line 20) records which jobs were launched
- **Update needed:** Only record jobs that were actually created (those passing `when` condition)

**Phase Completion Tracking:**
- **File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/run_execution_reconciler.go` (per metrics context)
- Jobs are tracked in `JobCompletions []RunAttemptJobCompletion` (run_api.go:38)
- Phase advances when all **registered** jobs reach terminal state (contract: line 94-95 of features/workflow-execution/contract.md)
- **Key change:** Track which jobs were registered vs. which are skipped; phase advance waits only for registered jobs

---

### 3. **Existing `skip_when_preserve_test_env` Implementation Footprint**

**A. Schema & Validation:**
- **Defined:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/read_api.go:157`
- **Validation:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/workflow_write_api.go:688-698`
  - Lines 688-698: Enforces teardown-only, mutually exclusive with verify/evidence_gate

**B. Dispatch/Launch Path:**
- **Checked:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/completion_api.go:1036`
  - Condition: `targetPhase.SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv && run.PreserveTestEnv`
  - Action: Appends attempt (line 1037) + stamps skipped (line 1040)
  - **Does NOT launch jobs; synthesizes skip instead**

**C. Skip Synthesis:**
- **File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/store/store/postgres.go:8614-8639`
- **Function:** `StampLatestAttemptSkipped()` (line 8614)
- **What it does:** 
  - Patches latest attempt with `conclusion="skipped"`, `decision="advance"` (lines 8625-8628)
  - Sets `completed_at` timestamp (line 8629)
  - Workflow advances past phase (line 1047 in completion_api.go)

**D. Store persistence:**
- **Columns:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/store/store/postgres.go:2139`
  - `SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv bool` (snapshot on each read)
- **Reads:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/store/store/postgres.go:3038, 3268`

**Pattern to Replicate for Job-Level When:**
- Evaluate condition at launch time (like checking `PreserveTestEnv`)
- Skip job creation if false
- Synthesize skipped job+step records so dashboard renders them
- Phase completion waits only for jobs that were registered

---

### 4. **Output Substitution & Missing Key Handling**

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/domain/phaserefs/phaserefs.go`

**Reference Parser:** `Parse()` (lines 24-31)
- Pattern: `${{ phases.<name>.outputs.<key> }}`
- Returns: `Ref{Phase, Key}` or false

**Validation:** `Validate()` (lines 34-74)
- Walks all phases
- **Error behavior (lines 98-107):** Returns error if output key not declared by upstream phase
- **Error message:** "phase %q input %q refs %s.outputs.%q; phase posted outputs %v" (line 100-107)

**Substitution:** `Substitute()` (lines 77-112)
- **Input:** phase's inputs map + captured `priorOutputs map[string]map[string]string`
- **Missing key behavior (lines 98-107):** Returns error, NOT empty string
- **Error propagated to:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/completion_api.go:1089` via `phaserefs.Substitute()`

**For Skipped Job Outputs:**
- Need new semantics: skipped job outputs resolve to empty string (not error)
- **Implementation site:** When recording skipped jobs, synthesize phase outputs with empty values for each declared output

---

### 5. **State Projection: Skipped Jobs & Steps for Dashboard**

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/graph_api.go:2140-2178`

**Step State Mapping (lines 2145-2162):**
- `"step_skipped"` event → `step.State = "skipped"` (line 2154)
- Used for dynamic expansion steps

**Synthesized Skipped Step Example:** lines 2207-2216
- When `dynamic_expansion` has `item_count=0`, synthetic step created:
  - `State: "skipped"`, slug derived, group metadata attached
  - **Pattern:** Create step objects without consuming native events

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/store/store/postgres.go:7750-7838`

**Skipped Phase Synthesis (lines 7752-7838):**
- Lines 7786-7823: When a phase is skipped, mark `phase["state"] = "skipped"`
- Lines 7830-7838: Mark all jobs in phase as skipped + all steps in each job as skipped
- **Synthesized records are written to projection map, not the native event stream**

**For Job-Level When Skip:**
- When job condition evaluates to false:
  1. Create job record with `state: "skipped"` (never created in k8s)
  2. Create synthetic step records for all declared steps with `state: "skipped"`
  3. Write to run projection so dashboard renders the skipped job/step hierarchy

---

### 6. **Registration-Time Template Validation Pattern**

**File:** `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/workflow_write_api.go:840-878`

**Function:** `validateDispatchInputRefs()` (line 846)
- Walks `phases[] → jobs[] → checkout.ref + extra_checkouts[].ref + workflow_ref`
- Uses regex: `runInputRefPattern` from `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/run_inputs.go:17`
  - Pattern: `${{ inputs.([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_-]*) }}`
- **Validation logic (lines 847-857):**
  - Extracts input key from ref
  - Checks against `declared map[string]DispatchInputSpec`
  - Returns error if undeclared: "refs ${{ inputs.%s }} but dispatch_inputs does not declare %q"

**For `when` Expression Validation:**
- Create similar walker for `job.When` field
- Support grammar: `${{ vars.X }}` for workflow-level variables (lines 39-40 of PhaseSpec context)
- Also support: plain boolean literals (`"true"`, `"false"`) for hardcoded conditions
- **Validation:** Declare that `when` field may reference:
  - Workflow-level `vars` map (new PhaseSpec field)
  - Run-time inputs: `${{ inputs.X }}`
  - Phase outputs from prior phases: `${{ phases.X.outputs.Y }}`

---

### 7. **Expression Evaluation Beyond String Substitution**

**Current State:** No equality/negation operators exist.

**Substitution is pure string replacement:**
- `/workspace/glimmung/internal/server/run_inputs.go:60-71` — `resolveRunInputTemplate()`
  - Regex matches, extracts key, returns value or error
  - **No operators, no conditionals**

- `/workspace/glimmung/internal/domain/phaserefs/phaserefs.go:77-112` — `Substitute()`
  - Maps phase ref to captured output string
  - Returns error or the literal string value
  - **No comparison logic**

**For Job-Level When:**
- Design choice: Keep expression language minimal
- Support:
  1. **String equality:** `${{ vars.CONDITION }} == "value"` (not operators yet, string match only)
  2. **Plain literals:** `true` / `false` (hardcoded skip conditions)
  3. **Variable reference:** `${{ vars.X }}` (substitute and use truthiness: empty string = false, non-empty = true)
- **Evaluation site:** New function in `native_launcher.go` before job manifest creation
- **Pattern:** Parse `when` field, resolve all `${{ ... }}` refs, evaluate as string equality or truthiness

---

## Summary Table

| Surface | File:Lines | Current | Addition Needed |
|---------|-----------|---------|-----------------|
| **Job Struct** | read_api.go:166-181 | NativeJobSpec | Add `When string` field |
| **Phase Struct** | read_api.go:131-158 | PhaseSpec | Add `Vars map[string]string` field |
| **Schema Validation** | workflow_write_api.go:608-878 | ValidateWorkflowRegister() | Validate `when` refs (follow line 846 pattern) |
| **Job Launch Loop** | native_launcher.go:220-237 | Iterate all jobs, create manifest | Evaluate `when` condition; skip if false |
| **Job Recording** | native_launch_context.go:20-44 | Record launched jobs | Only record non-skipped jobs |
| **Skip Synthesis** | completion_api.go:1036-1052 | Synthesize skipped phase attempt | Synthesize skipped job+step records (per postgres.go:7750-7838 pattern) |
| **Output Substitution** | phaserefs/phaserefs.go:77-112 | Error on missing key | For skipped jobs, resolve to empty string (new path) |
| **Store Persistence** | store/postgres.go:8614-8639 | StampLatestAttemptSkipped() | No change needed (same pattern) |
| **Dashboard Projection** | graph_api.go:2140-2178, store/postgres.go:7750-7838 | Step state events | Write synthesized job/step skip records (per line 7752+ pattern) |
| **Expression Eval** | N/A | String substitution only | New: Simple truthiness eval for `when` conditions |

payload.output:
1	# Migration Policy
2	
3	When migrating off an old system, the old system must be deleted end to end.
4	Compatibility is prohibited.
5	
6	This is not a preference or a default that can be overridden by agent caution.
7	It is the migration contract for this repository.
8	
9	## Completion Standard
10	
11	A migrated path is not complete until all of these are true:
12	
13	- no live routes
14	- no UI controls or links
15	- no allocator or executor branches
16	- no fallback defaults
17	- no old behavior tests
18	- no docs saying the old path is supported
19	- no runtime reads whose purpose is to keep old behavior working
20	
21	Unknown callers are unsupported. Known old callers are unsupported. Old data
22	does not justify runtime support.
23	
24	If removal exposes another dependency on the old system, delete that dependency
25	too. If the task cannot be completed, stop with a blocker report naming the
26	exact remaining old dependency.
27	
28	Do not add a compatibility layer. Do not add a fallback. Do not keep a
29	read-only runtime path.
30	
31	## Agent Checklist
32	
33	When asked to complete a migration, search for the old system's names, routes,
34	types, feature flags, tests, docs, UI labels, and storage behavior. Remove every
35	live path.
36	
37	Treat `legacy`, `compatibility`, `fallback`, `temporary`, and `exception` as
38	deletion targets, not design options.
39	
40	Tests should fail if the retired path is reintroduced into live code.
41

1	# Quality Timeframes
2	
3	This is a long-term learning, craft, and portfolio operating mode. The default
4	engineering timeframe is years, not a sprint, a demo, or a quick production
5	patch. When a decision can be made at short, medium, or heavy weight, choose
6	the heavy long-term solution unless the user explicitly asks for a temporary
7	experiment.
8	
9	This document is a decision policy for agents and humans working on substantial
10	technical projects. It sets the quality bar and the expected time horizon.
11	When a project also has migration, architecture, inspiration, or design-system
12	guidance, those documents are part of the definition of done.
13	
14	## Default Timeframe
15	
16	Assume the user wants the robust version:
17	
18	- Prefer complete architecture over quick relief.
19	- Prefer settled contracts over compatibility layers.
20	- Prefer durable state over process memory.
21	- Prefer observable systems over "it probably works."
22	- Prefer finishing the hardening now over creating a follow-up list.
23	- Prefer learning the right technology over avoiding it to save time.
24	
25	Short-term fixes create long-term work when the work is already committed to
26	the full solution. Do not choose a light or medium implementation because it is
27	faster unless the user explicitly says speed is the priority for this task.
28	
29	## Done Standard
30	
31	A feature is not done when the happy path works. It is done when the user can
32	step away from that feature and focus on another complete feature without
33	worrying about hidden hardening debt.
34	
35	For substantial work, include these before calling it complete:
36	
37	- The durable data model or state ownership is explicit.
38	- Runtime behavior survives realistic disconnects, retries, restarts, and
39	  rollouts within the stated product boundary.
40	- User-visible state is not inferred from transient local optimism when a
41	  durable source exists.
42	- Failure, timeout, cancellation, and retry states are designed and visible.
43	- Observability exists for the bugs a user would otherwise have to guess about.
44	- Tests cover the contract, not only the implementation detail.
45	- Docs describe the final behavior and remove obsolete behavior.
46	- Migration guards prevent old paths from returning.
47	- Cost and scaling implications are understood, measured, or deliberately
48	  bounded.
49	
50	If any of these are missing, either finish them in the same work or stop with a
51	blocker report that names the exact remaining dependency. Do not merge first
52	and leave the user to ask what remains.
53	
54	## Planning Rule
55	
56	When planning, do not present a minimal fix as the default. Start from the
57	long-term design and break it into chunks only when the chunks are independently
58	safe.
59	
60	Acceptable chunking:
61	
62	- A sequence of PRs where each PR leaves the system in a coherent state.
63	- A preparatory refactor that has its own verification and removes risk for the
64	  full solution.
65	- A schema or infrastructure step that is inactive until the complete path is
66	  ready, provided it does not preserve old behavior or add compatibility.
67	
68	Unacceptable chunking:
69	
70	- Shipping the easy UI change and leaving the durable model for later.
71	- Keeping polling, sockets, fallback reads, or old routes as a safety blanket
72	  after a migration.
73	- Adding logs while deferring the metrics needed to operate the feature.
74	- Marking a control action successful before the durable system confirms it.
75	- Leaving "future hardening" as an unowned list after merge.
76	
77	If the full solution is too large for one PR, write the full plan first. The
78	plan must name every stage needed for the final state and identify which stages
79	are required before the feature can be considered done.
80	
81	## Communication Rule
82	
83	Be explicit about quality tradeoffs before implementation and before merge.
84	
85	Before implementation:
86	
87	- State the long-term endpoint, not only the immediate patch.
88	- Identify any tempting short-term shortcut and whether it is being rejected.
89	- Call out operational risks such as cost, observability, rollout behavior, and
90	  stuck states.
91	
92	Before merge:
93	
94	- Say whether the feature is complete by this document's standard.
95	- If it is not complete, do not frame the remaining work as optional
96	  robustness. Name it as unfinished scope.
97	- Do not make the user discover a 5-10 item hardening list after merge.
98	
99	## Relationship To Urgency
100	
101	Urgency changes sequencing, not the quality bar. If there is a production
102	incident, it is acceptable to make a small stabilizing change only when it is
103	clearly labeled as incident containment and paired with the complete fix plan.
104	Outside that explicit emergency mode, optimize for the full solution.
105	
106	## Review Heuristics
107	
108	Challenge work that has these smells:
109	
110	- "Just in case" fallback paths.
111	- Compatibility for unknown callers.
112	- Runtime reads whose purpose is to keep an old behavior alive.
113	- Local UI state that can contradict durable state.
114	- Polling loops without a cost and scale story.
115	- Missing counters for user-trust failures.
116	- A feature that only works until reload, reconnect, restart, or rollout.
117	- A PR description that says what changed but not what is now complete.
118	
119	The preferred outcome is fewer, more complete features. Heavy is the default.
120

# Feature Contracts

The repo-wide policy docs describe the quality bar:

- [migration-policy.md](../migration-policy.md)
- [quality-timeframes.md](../quality-timeframes.md)
- [go-migration-contracts.md](../go-migration-contracts.md)

Feature contracts translate those rules into concrete, reviewable invariants
for the parts of Glimmung that can visibly lie to users, reviewers, agents, or
operators.

Read the relevant contract before planning or implementing substantial work in
that area. A PR that touches a contracted feature should name the contract and
show evidence that the contract still holds. Evidence can be a unit test,
integration test, browser observation, route inventory test, workflow
registration rejection, metric, log, run report, or direct runtime observation,
depending on the risk.

## Contracts

- [Auth And API Surface](auth-and-api/contract.md)
- [Dashboard And Styleguide](dashboard-and-styleguide/contract.md)
- [Issues And Runs](issues-and-runs/contract.md)
- [Observability And Evidence](observability-and-evidence/contract.md)
- [Review Surfaces](review-surfaces/contract.md)
- [Test Slots](test-slots/contract.md)
- [Workflow Execution](workflow-execution/contract.md)

## When To Add A Contract

Add or expand a feature contract when a feature:

- can show state that contradicts Postgres, Kubernetes, GitHub, or callback
  state;
- crosses browser, MCP, workflow runner, database, Kubernetes, or GitHub
  boundaries;
- depends on live streams, reconnect, retry, rollout, or async recovery;
- has controls where "appeared to work" is different from "durably worked";
- has already produced a user-trust or operator-trust bug.

1	# Workflow Execution Contract
2	
3	This contract applies to workflow registration, schema snapshots, phase/job
4	shape, native Kubernetes job launch, managed evidence gates, callback tokens,
5	and workflow sync helpers.
6	
7	## Product Model
8	
9	A Workflow is the registered automation shape for a project. It gives agents a
10	precise lane: prepare capacity, do work, verify the result, and clean up. The
11	workflow graph should be legible before dispatch and should remain reconstructable
12	after registration changes.
13	
14	## Sources Of Truth
15	
16	- Postgres `workflows` owns logical workflow registrations and current schema
17	  pointers. Its `control_pins` column is operator-owned: only the dedicated
18	  pin/unpin endpoints write it, never registration.
19	- Postgres `workflow_control_events` owns the append-only attribution ledger
20	  for control-plane writes (register, patch, pin, unpin, delete) — schema
21	  rows are content-addressed and cannot carry who-moved-the-pointer history.
22	- Historical workflow schemas referenced by runs own projection for past
23	  cycles.
24	- `docs/workflow-shape.md` owns required phases, linear topology, job
25	  concurrency, evidence gate semantics, and path-typed identity.
26	- Native Kubernetes Jobs own execution process state while running.
27	- Native job event rows own hot execution telemetry.
28	- Workflow import/sync inputs are admin conveniences only. They are not
29	  required in consumer repositories and are never the runtime source of truth.
30	
31	## Migration Rules
32	
33	- Do not make repo workflow files the dispatch source of truth.
34	- Do not register executor kinds other than `k8s_job`. Review gates are
35	  `k8s_job` phases with `purpose: review_gate`; a review-gate phase must
36	  declare exactly one `pr_merge` primitive job and no other jobs. The
37	  `pr_merge` primitive must live inside a `purpose: review_gate` phase and
38	  nowhere else.
39	- Do not add phase fan-in, fan-out, job-level dependencies, or non-linear DAG
40	  behavior without replacing this contract.
41	- Do not allow project-owned arbitrary gate jobs to stand in for the managed
42	  evidence gate.
43	- Do not delete historical schemas still referenced by run history.
44	- Do not start a workflow-execution background reconciler (run queue,
45	  dispatch timeout, completion sweep, native Job inspection, etc.) outside
46	  the `settings.ControlPlaneLoopsEnabled` gate in `cmd/glimmung-go/main.go`.
47	  The control-plane isolation boundary belongs to the
48	  [Test Slots contract](../test-slots/contract.md); a workflow-execution
49	  reconciler that ignores it lets a hot-swapped slot binary race the prod
50	  glimmung Deployment on the same runs, Postgres rows, and `glimmung-runs`
51	  Kubernetes Jobs.
52	
53	## Live Behavior
54	
55	- Registration rejects missing entry, verify, or teardown cleanup phases.
56	- Registration rejects invalid dependencies, duplicate phases, duplicate job
57	  IDs, invalid inputs, and unsupported executor kinds before they become a
58	  runtime contract.
59	- A verification phase must declare `recycle_policy` explicitly; registration
60	  rejects a nil policy naming the phase. There is no implicit platform
61	  attempt count for silence to inherit.
62	- Every workflow write flows through one choke point that enforces operator
63	  control pins: a pinned target's incoming value is discarded in favor of the
64	  pinned value before the schema hash is computed, the override is reported
65	  on the response and ledger, and a pin whose target phase is absent from the
66	  incoming registration rejects the registration. Patches naming a pinned
67	  target are rejected with the pinner, reason, and unpin remediation. No
68	  write path may mutate the workflow payload without minting a schema and a
69	  ledger event.
70	- A `verify=true` phase is a bounded verification phase whose concrete shape is
71	  selected by the persisted workflow constraint
72	  `constraints.verification.shape`. Supported shapes are `single_job`,
73	  `bounded_case_jobs`, and `dynamic_step_group`; code validates the selected
74	  primitive, but the workflow row owns which one applies. `dynamic_step_group`
75	  declares exactly one sequential verification job with one dynamic test-case
76	  block whose `dynamic_group.max_items` is at or below 10.
77	  `bounded_case_jobs` declares ten jobs named `verify-case-01` through
78	  `verify-case-10`; every case job sets `timeout_seconds` at or below 600
79	  seconds, and unused slots complete successfully with no-op case results.
80	- Jobs inside one phase launch in parallel and complete independently.
81	- A step-scoped fail-closed abort is represented by a typed `step_aborted`
82	  native event and a durable aborted step state. A failed or aborted job whose
83	  cause is step-scoped must not project with every step succeeded or
84	  not-started.
85	- A phase/job dispatch failure before a Kubernetes Job exists is represented as
86	  a failed workflow-owned `dispatch` step. Declared workflow steps remain
87	  `not_started`; the synthetic dispatch step owns the terminal failure instead
88	  of leaving the human UI without a failed node.
89	- `touchpoint_gate` is a gated native phase name, not an executor kind:
90	  reaching the `purpose: review_gate` phase creates a durable parked `k8s_job`
91	  attempt at the human decision boundary, and approve later releases that same
92	  attempt's managed `pr_merge` job through the ordinary native event,
93	  completion, watcher, and recovery paths.
94	- Phase advancement happens only after all registered jobs in the phase reach
95	  terminal callback state.
96	- Verification phases preserve verification statuses, reasons, evidence refs,
97	  and typed evidence artifacts in the phase completion. Glimmung synthesizes
98	  the phase output `verification` so the managed evidence gate has a stable
99	  JSON verdict.
100	  Multi-job verification phases aggregate per-job verification data before
101	  synthesizing that phase output.
102	- Dynamic verification groups are runtime-expanded inside one managed job from
103	  bounded plan outputs (`test_cases_json` or `test_cases_count`, plus
104	  group-specific aliases). Expanded case steps are emitted as durable native
105	  step events with concrete slugs and group metadata; template steps must not
106	  remain the only visible execution record after expansion.
107	- Evidence verification gates are canonicalized into managed Glimmung runner
108	  jobs.
109	- Dispatch may include bounded string `inputs`. These are durable run facts,
110	  persisted on the Run as `run_inputs`, copied into every native lease's
111	  metadata, exposed to native pods as `GLIMMUNG_RUN_INPUT_*`, and preserved
112	  across recycle attempts. Native `checkout.ref` and `extra_checkouts[].ref`
113	  may use exact run-input templates such as `${{ inputs.git_ref }}`; the
114	  launcher resolves them before emitting the native runner job spec so the
115	  runner receives a concrete ref.
116	- Runs use the workflow schema snapshot captured at run/cycle creation, not a
117	  later logical workflow update.
118	
119	## Failure And Recovery
120	
121	- A failed native Job produces durable job/phase failure state through the
122	  completion callback path, not a retired failure route.
123	- Teardown phases (`purpose: teardown`) are verdict-neutral. A teardown job's
124	  outcome never sets the run verdict: a failed teardown must not abort an
125	  otherwise-passing run, and must not override or mask the primary phase's
126	  verdict. The decision engine advances the cleanup chain regardless of a
127	  teardown conclusion, and terminal-cause attribution skips teardown attempts.
128	  The failed teardown remains visible on its own job/step state. Teardown jobs
129	  carry a bounded `backoffLimit` so a transient pod-start failure self-heals;
130	  producer/verify jobs keep `backoffLimit=0` (fail fast).
131	- A failed dispatch produces durable phase/job/step failure state through run
132	  terminal finalization and run graph projection, including historical rows
133	  whose child jobs or steps were previously stamped `skipped`.
134	- Callback-token validation failure must not mutate unrelated runs or phases.
135	- Workflow update failure should leave the previous logical workflow pointer
136	  intact.
137	- Service restart must preserve the ability to project active and historical
138	  cycles from schema refs and run ledgers.
139	
140	## Observability
141	
142	- Native event streams should identify project, issue, run, cycle, phase, job,
143	  step, conclusion, and relevant log tail or archive link.
144	- A pod that dies for a pod-level reason (OOMKilled, Evicted) must surface that
145	  reason, not just the Job condition. The Kubernetes Job condition collapses to
146	  `BackoffLimitExceeded` for any `backoffLimit=0` pod failure, so Glimmung reads
147	  the pod's `containerStatuses[].state.terminated.reason` / `status.reason` on
148	  terminal failure and refines the terminal reason to `oom_killed` / `evicted`
149	  (and keeps the raw reason in the human summary). This applies to outer Jobs
150	  and inner per-case Jobs alike.
151	- Registration failures should name the exact invalid phase, dependency, input,
152	  job, or unsupported kind.
153	- Run graph projection should make schema mismatch or missing schema failures
154	  explicit.
155	
156	## Acceptance Checks
157	
158	- Workflow shape changes include registration validation tests.
159	- Control-pin changes include tests for pin enforcement on re-registration,
160	  pinned-patch rejection, the closed pin-target grammar, and ledger/actor
161	  attribution on the write surface.
162	- Native launcher/callback changes include multi-job phase behavior when the
163	  change can affect phase completion.
164	- Dispatch-input changes include tests for run persistence, native lease/env
165	  propagation, checkout-ref resolution, missing-input failure, and recycle
166	  preservation when applicable.
167	- Verification-case shape changes include tests for required case IDs, bounded
168	  timeouts, and synthesized aggregate verification output.
169	- Gate changes prove managed gate canonicalization and terminal behavior.
170	- Teardown-routing changes prove a failed teardown stays verdict-neutral: it
171	  does not abort a passing run and does not become the run's terminal cause
172	  over a failed primary phase.
173	- Sync-helper changes prove they do not bypass registration validation.
174	- Historical run projection still works when the logical workflow changes.
175

type workflowDoc struct {
	ID                  string                     `json:"id"`
	Kind                string                     `json:"kind,omitempty"`
	Project             string                     `json:"project"`
	Name                string                     `json:"name"`
	SchemaRef           string                     `json:"schema_ref,omitempty"`
	Phases              []phaseDoc                 `json:"phases"`
	PR                  prDoc                      `json:"pr"`
	Budget              budgetDoc                  `json:"budget"`
	Constraints         server.WorkflowConstraints `json:"constraints,omitempty"`
	DefaultRequirements map[string]any             `json:"defaultRequirements"`
	DispatchInputs      []server.DispatchInputSpec `json:"dispatchInputs,omitempty"`
	Metadata            map[string]any             `json:"metadata"`
	CreatedAt           string                     `json:"createdAt"`
}

type leaseDoc struct {
	ID                 string         `json:"id"`
	Kind               string         `json:"kind"`
	LeaseNumber        *int           `json:"leaseNumber"`
	Project            string         `json:"project"`
	Workflow           *string        `json:"workflow"`
	Host               *string        `json:"host"`
	State              string         `json:"state"`
	Requirements       map[string]any `json:"requirements"`
	Metadata           map[string]any `json:"metadata"`
	RequestedAt        string         `json:"requestedAt"`
	AssignedAt         string         `json:"assignedAt"`
	ReleasedAt         string         `json:"releasedAt"`
	TTLSeconds         int            `json:"ttlSeconds"`

func workflowFromDoc(doc workflowDoc) server.Workflow {
	phases := make([]server.PhaseSpec, 0, len(doc.Phases))
	for _, phase := range doc.Phases {
		phases = append(phases, phaseFromDoc(phase))
	}

	return server.Workflow{
		ID:                  firstNonEmpty(doc.ID, doc.Name),
		Project:             doc.Project,
		Name:                doc.Name,
		SchemaRef:           firstNonEmpty(doc.SchemaRef, workflowSchemaRef(doc)),
		Phases:              phases,
		PR:                  prFromDoc(doc.PR),
		Budget:              budget.Config{Total: defaultBudgetTotal(doc.Budget.Total)},
		Constraints:         doc.Constraints,
		DefaultRequirements: mapOrEmpty(doc.DefaultRequirements),
		DispatchInputs:      cloneDispatchInputs(doc.DispatchInputs),
		Metadata:            mapOrEmpty(doc.Metadata),
		CreatedAt:           parseTimeOrNow(doc.CreatedAt),
	}
}

func leaseFromDoc(doc leaseDoc) server.Lease {
	return server.Lease{
		ID:                 doc.ID,
		Kind:               doc.Kind,
		LeaseNumber:        doc.LeaseNumber,
		Project:            doc.Project,
		Workflow:           doc.Workflow,
		Host:               doc.Host,

func workflowDocFromRegister(req server.WorkflowRegister, createdAt string) workflowDoc {
	phases := make([]phaseDoc, 0, len(req.Phases))
	for _, phase := range req.Phases {
		phases = append(phases, phaseDocFromSpec(phase))
	}
	req.Constraints = server.CanonicalWorkflowConstraints(req)
	return workflowDoc{
		ID:                  req.Name,
		Project:             req.Project,
		Name:                req.Name,
		Phases:              phases,
		PR:                  prDocFromSpec(req.PR),
		Budget:              budgetDoc{Total: defaultBudgetTotal(req.Budget.Total)},
		Constraints:         req.Constraints,
		DefaultRequirements: mapOrEmpty(req.DefaultRequirements),
		DispatchInputs:      cloneDispatchInputs(req.DispatchInputs),
		Metadata:            mapOrEmpty(req.Metadata),
		CreatedAt:           createdAt,
	}
}

func workflowRegisterFromDoc(doc workflowDoc) server.WorkflowRegister {
	phases := make([]server.PhaseSpec, 0, len(doc.Phases))
	for _, phase := range doc.Phases {
		phases = append(phases, phaseFromDoc(phase))
	}
	return server.WorkflowRegister{
		Project:             doc.Project,
		Name:                doc.Name,
		Phases:              phases,
		PR:                  prFromDoc(doc.PR),
		Budget:              budget.Config{Total: defaultBudgetTotal(doc.Budget.Total)},
		Constraints:         doc.Constraints,
		DefaultRequirements: mapOrEmpty(doc.DefaultRequirements),
		DispatchInputs:      cloneDispatchInputs(doc.DispatchInputs),
		Metadata:            mapOrEmpty(doc.Metadata),
	}
}

	ID               string              `json:"id"`

type nativeStepDoc struct {
	Slug             string                   `json:"slug"`
	Title            *string                  `json:"title"`
	Type             string                   `json:"type,omitempty"`
	Run              string                   `json:"run,omitempty"`
	Agent            *agentStepDoc            `json:"agent,omitempty"`
	Shell            string                   `json:"shell,omitempty"`
	WorkingDirectory string                   `json:"workingDirectory,omitempty"`
	Env              map[string]string        `json:"env,omitempty"`
	Group            string                   `json:"group,omitempty"`
	GroupTitle       *string                  `json:"groupTitle,omitempty"`
func phaseDocFromSpec(phase server.PhaseSpec) phaseDoc {
	phase = server.CanonicalNativePhase(phase)
	jobs := make([]nativeJobDoc, 0, len(phase.Jobs))
	for _, job := range phase.Jobs {
		jobs = append(jobs, nativeJobDocFromSpec(job))
	}
	return phaseDoc{
		Name:                     phase.Name,
		Kind:                     firstNonEmpty(phase.Kind, "k8s_job"),
		RunOn:                    phase.RunOn,
		Purpose:                  phase.Purpose,
		WorkflowFilename:         phase.WorkflowFilename,
		WorkflowRef:              firstNonEmpty(phase.WorkflowRef, "main"),
		Inputs:                   stringMapOrEmpty(phase.Inputs),
		Outputs:                  sliceOrEmpty(phase.Outputs),
		Requirements:             mapOrEmpty(phase.Requirements),
		Verify:                   phase.Verify,
		RecyclePolicy:            recyclePolicyDocFromSpec(phase.RecyclePolicy),
		EvidenceVerificationGate: phase.EvidenceVerificationGate,
		DependsOn:                sliceOrEmpty(phase.DependsOn),
		Jobs:                     jobs,
		SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv:  phase.SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv,
	}
}

func nativeJobDocFromSpec(job server.NativeJobSpec) nativeJobDoc {
	steps := make([]nativeStepDoc, 0, len(job.Steps))
	for _, step := range job.Steps {
		steps = append(steps, nativeStepDoc{
			Slug:             step.Slug,
			Title:            step.Title,
			Type:             step.Type,
			Run:              step.Run,
			Agent:            agentStepDocFromSpec(step.Agent),
			Shell:            step.Shell,
			WorkingDirectory: step.WorkingDirectory,
			Env:              stringMapOrEmpty(step.Env),
			Group:            step.Group,
			GroupTitle:       step.GroupTitle,
			DynamicGroup:     step.DynamicGroup,
		})
	}
	extraCheckouts := make([]nativeCheckoutDoc, 0, len(job.ExtraCheckouts))
	for _, checkout := range job.ExtraCheckouts {
		extraCheckouts = append(extraCheckouts, nativeCheckoutDocFromSpec(checkout))
	}
	return nativeJobDoc{
		ID:               job.ID,
		Name:             job.Name,
		Primitive:        job.Primitive,
		Image:            job.Image,
		Command:          sliceOrEmpty(job.Command),
		Args:             sliceOrEmpty(job.Args),
		Env:              stringMapOrEmpty(job.Env),
		Steps:            steps,
		TimeoutSeconds:   job.TimeoutSeconds,
		Managed:          job.Managed,
		Checkout:         nativeCheckoutDocPtrFromSpec(job.Checkout),
		ExtraCheckouts:   extraCheckouts,
func phaseFromDoc(doc phaseDoc) server.PhaseSpec {
	jobs := make([]server.NativeJobSpec, 0, len(doc.Jobs))
	for _, job := range doc.Jobs {
		jobs = append(jobs, jobFromDoc(job))
	}
	return server.PhaseSpec{
		Name:                     doc.Name,
		Kind:                     firstNonEmpty(doc.Kind, "k8s_job"),
		RunOn:                    doc.RunOn,
		Purpose:                  doc.Purpose,
		WorkflowFilename:         doc.WorkflowFilename,
		WorkflowRef:              firstNonEmpty(doc.WorkflowRef, "main"),
		Inputs:                   stringMapOrEmpty(doc.Inputs),
		Outputs:                  sliceOrEmpty(doc.Outputs),
		Requirements:             doc.Requirements,
		Verify:                   doc.Verify,
		RecyclePolicy:            recyclePolicyFromDoc(doc.RecyclePolicy),
		EvidenceVerificationGate: doc.EvidenceVerificationGate,
		DependsOn:                sliceOrEmpty(doc.DependsOn),
		Jobs:                     jobs,
		SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv:  doc.SkipWhenPreserveTestEnv,
	}
}

func jobFromDoc(doc nativeJobDoc) server.NativeJobSpec {

			GroupTitle:       step.GroupTitle,
			DynamicGroup:     step.DynamicGroup,
		})
	}
	extraCheckouts := make([]server.NativeCheckoutSpec, 0, len(doc.ExtraCheckouts))
	for _, checkout := range doc.ExtraCheckouts {
		extraCheckouts = append(extraCheckouts, nativeCheckoutFromDoc(checkout))
	}
	return server.NativeJobSpec{
		ID:               doc.ID,
		Name:             doc.Name,
		Primitive:        doc.Primitive,
		Image:            doc.Image,
		Command:          sliceOrEmpty(doc.Command),
		Args:             sliceOrEmpty(doc.Args),

	ReadProjectForDispatch(ctx context.Context, project string) (Project, error)
	ReadProjectGitHubRepo(ctx context.Context, project string) (string, error)
	ReadIssueForDispatch(ctx context.Context, project string, issueNumber int) (IssueDispatchData, error)
	GetWorkflowByName(ctx context.Context, project, name string) (*Workflow, error)
	ListProjectWorkflows(ctx context.Context, project string) ([]Workflow, error)
	ClaimIssueLock(ctx context.Context, project string, issueNumber int, holderID string, ttlSeconds int) error
	ReleaseIssueLock(ctx context.Context, project string, issueNumber int, holderID string)
	CreateRun(ctx context.Context, req CreateRunRequest) (CreatedRun, error)
	StartRunCycle(ctx context.Context, req StartRunCycleRequest) (int, error)
	AcquireLease(ctx context.Context, req LeaseAcquireRequest) (Lease, error)
	ReadLeaseByRef(ctx context.Context, project, ref string) (Lease, error)
	CancelLeaseByRef(ctx context.Context, project, ref string) (CancelLeaseResult, error)
	AbortRunByID(ctx context.Context, project, runID, reason string) (AbortRunResult, error)
}

input.new_string:
## Conditional Phases And Jobs (`when` / `vars`)

Phases and jobs may declare a `when` condition, evaluated **server-side at
phase dispatch, before any Kubernetes Job exists**. A false condition spends
zero compute (GitHub Actions `if:` / GitLab `rules:` parity): the platform
synthesizes durable skipped records instead of launching, and the dashboard
renders the declared-but-skipped leg. The workflow shape stays total — the
skip *is* the documentation of the toggle.

```yaml
vars:
  feature_type: effect
phases:
  - name: llm-work
    jobs:
      - id: llm-test-plan
        when: "${{ vars.feature_type }} != 'effect'"   # skipped for effect runs
      - id: llm-implement                               # unconditional
  - name: cleanup_early
    purpose: teardown
    when: "${{ run.preserve_test_env }} == 'false'"
```

The grammar is closed — a routing condition, not a scripting surface:
`true` | `false` | `<term> ==|!= <term>`, where a term is `${{ vars.<key> }}`,
`${{ inputs.<key> }}` (a declared dispatch input), `${{ run.preserve_test_env }}`,
or a literal. Comparison is exact string equality after resolution.
Registration validates every ref against the declared `vars` map and
`dispatch_inputs`, and rejects:

- `when` on a verification or review-gate phase or its jobs — gates run;
- `when` on an entry phase (no `depends_on`) — fresh cycles and recycle
  landings need a defined start;
- a phase whose every job is conditional — at least one job stays
  unconditional so a launched phase always launches something (whole-phase
  conditions belong on the phase-level `when`).

Semantics:

- **Phase-level skip**: a false phase condition synthesizes a `skipped`
  attempt with the resolved condition as the reason, and the workflow
  advances past it like a success. Every job whose condition is false when
  its siblings all skip collapses to the same phase-level skip.
- **Job-level skip**: a false job condition writes a synthesized `skipped`
  job completion (pre-satisfying the expected-job set, so the phase
  completes on launched siblings alone) and skipped job/step execution
  records. No Job object, secret, or pod is ever created.
- **Skipped outputs are empty**: downstream
  `${{ phases.X.outputs.Y }}` refs against a phase with skipped legs resolve
  to the empty string when the output was never published — consumers handle
  absence, exactly like skipped-job outputs in GitHub Actions. Phases with
  no skips keep the strict fail-closed behavior.
- **Verdict-neutral**: a skipped job neither degrades a succeeding phase nor
  masks a failing sibling.
- `vars` is registration-owned workflow identity (part of the content-hashed
  schema), not a per-dispatch knob; per-dispatch routing uses
  `${{ inputs.* }}`.

## Verification Case Shapes

input.body:
## What

Phases and jobs may declare a **`when` condition** — evaluated **server-side at phase dispatch, before any Kubernetes Job exists**. A false condition spends zero compute (GHA `if:` / GitLab `rules:` parity): the platform synthesizes durable skipped records attributed to the resolved condition, and the declared-but-skipped leg renders in the run graph. The workflow shape stays total; the skip *is* the documentation of the toggle.

New registration surface:
- **`vars`** (workflow-level, content-hashed): registration-owned facts conditions reference — e.g. `vars: {feature_type: effect}`.
- **`when`** on phases and jobs. Closed grammar: `true | false | <term> ==|!= <term>`; terms are `${{ vars.<key> }}`, `${{ inputs.<key> }}` (declared dispatch inputs), `${{ run.preserve_test_env }}`, or literals. Exact string comparison; no truthiness, no boolean algebra.

## Mechanics

- **Phase-level skip** (false phase condition, or every-job-skip collapse): synthesized `skipped` attempt with the resolution trace as reason; workflow advances like a success. This **generalizes and replaces** the bespoke `skip_when_preserve_test_env` flag — registration now rejects the retired field with a pointer at the `when` form (migration guard test included).
- **Job-level skip**: synthesized `skipped` job completion written at dispatch — pre-satisfies the expected-job completion set so the phase completes on launched siblings alone — plus skipped job/step execution records. Launcher receives `SkipJobIDs` and creates no secret/manifest/Job. Launch results are now **typed** (`NativeLaunchedJob`) so skips can't shift the positional ID↔name mapping.
- **All launch sites honor conditions**: forward dispatch, initial cycle admission, recycle landing, synthetic dispatch. Review-gate release exempt by construction (validation forbids conditions on gates).
- **Skipped-output semantics**: `${{ phases.X.outputs.Y }}` against a phase with skipped legs resolves to `""` when unpublished; phases with no skips keep strict fail-closed substitution.
- **Verdict neutrality**: a skipped completion neither degrades a succeeding phase nor masks a failing sibling — this also fixes a latent aggregation-order bug where an early `skipped` conclusion (an advance conclusion) could win over a later `failure` and advance past a failed job.
- **Validation** (named errors): grammar + refs checked against declared `vars`/`dispatch_inputs`; `when` rejected on verification/review-gate phases+jobs (gates run), entry phases (runs need a defined start), and phases whose every job is conditional (≥1 job stays unconditional so a launched phase always launches something).

## Feature Contracts

- **Workflow Execution** — updated: live-behavior invariant for conditions/skip semantics, migration rule banning `skip_when_preserve_test_env` reintroduction, acceptance checks for when/vars changes. `docs/workflow-shape.md` gains the Conditional Phases And Jobs section and the cleanup_early text now describes the `when` form.

Evidence: `whenexpr` grammar/eval/validate unit tests; registration validation tests (accept, retired-field rejection, verify/entry/all-conditional rejections, undeclared-ref rejection, vars key grammar); forward-dispatch behavior tests (job-level skip records + SkipJobIDs + no whole-phase stamp; phase-level stamp with resolved reason; condition-holds no-skip); skip-neutral aggregation tests; expected-set pre-satisfaction test; skipped-output substitution tests. `go test ./...` green except pre-existing env-dependent `TestInstallAgentPostCommitReminder` (fails identically on clean main in this pod).

## Motivation

ambience#167's feature-type standing-case flow needs `llm-test-plan` declared-but-skipped at parity with major CI platforms — not a single cycle of compute on a skipped job (operator requirement). Consumers on day one: `ambience.default` (`vars.feature_type=effect`, `when` on llm-test-plan + issue-contract jobs) and `spirelens.default` (`cleanup_early` preserve-test-env fold-in). Both re-registrations follow merge+deploy.

payload.text:
Milestone update while both watchers run:

## Glimmung `when`/`vars` feature — built, tested, PR open

**[PR #764](https://github.com/romaine-life/glimmung/pull/764)** (+1231/−108 across 26 files), CI running. What shipped:

- **`whenexpr`** — closed condition grammar (`true|false|<term> ==|!= <term>` over `vars.*`, `inputs.*`, `run.preserve_test_env`), parse/validate/eval with resolution traces so every skip names exactly why
- **Zero-compute skips at all four launch sites** (forward dispatch, initial cycle admission, recycle landing, synthetic dispatch): false job condition → synthesized `skipped` job completion + skipped job/step records written at dispatch, **no Job object ever created**; false phase condition → synthesized skipped attempt, workflow advances past it
- **`skip_when_preserve_test_env` folded in and retired** per migration policy — registration rejects it with a pointer at the `when` replacement; guard test prevents reintroduction
- **GHA-parity output semantics** — skipped legs' unpublished outputs resolve to `""` downstream; no-skip phases keep fail-closed strictness
- **Typed launch results** — fixed the latent positional `launched[i]`↔`Jobs[i]` mapping fragility
- **Found and fixed a real masking bug along the way**: phase-conclusion aggregation let an early `skipped` conclusion (an *advance* conclusion) win over a later `failure` — a skipped sibling could have advanced a run past a failed implement. Now verdict-neutral, with tests pinning both directions
- Validation hardening: `when` rejected on verify/review-gate phases+jobs (gates run), entry phases, all-conditional phases (≥1 job stays unconditional); refs validated against declared `vars`/`dispatch_inputs` with exact locations
- Contract + `workflow-shape.md` updated; full suite green (one pre-existing env-dependent runner test fails identically on clean main)

The strongest validation: the migrated preserve-test-env test fixtures now pass **through the new generic mechanism** — the fold-in is behaviorally equivalent where it matters and stricter where it should be.

## Run 12.1 (ambience#167)

`llm-implement` still running at ~47m (codex-deep building paper-lanterns + PR CI wait + env rebuild — normal range). Skip-leg checkpoint already passed. Verify is next; monitor armed.

## Queue

1. PR #764 CI → merge → **deploy glimmung** (ArgoCD) 
2. Ambience wave 2 (prune issue-contract → declared `public_surface`, standing sourcing verify-side, delete my interim skip-leg)

# --- implement.sh ui_hint contract (feature types with a standing case) ---
(
  IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR="${TMP_DIR}/impl-evidence"
  mkdir -p "$IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR"
  export AMBIENCE_IMPLEMENT_SOURCE_ONLY=1
  export AMBIENCE_EVIDENCE_DIR="$IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR"
  export GLIMMUNG_INPUT_VALIDATION_URL="https://preview.test"
  export GLIMMUNG_INPUT_NAMESPACE="preview-ns"
  export GLIMMUNG_INPUT_IMAGE_TAG="git-aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"
  unset GLIMMUNG_STEP_SLUG

  # shellcheck source=glimmung-native/implement.sh
  source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/glimmung-native/implement.sh"

  # passing implementation with a valid ui_hint: finalize succeeds and emit
  # publishes the hint as its own phase output
  cat >"${IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR}/issue-agent-implementation.json" <<'JSON'
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "status": "pass",
  "summary": "added paper lanterns",
  "ui_hint": {"menu_label": "paper-lanterns", "route": "/dev/paper-lanterns"}
}
JSON
  export AMBIENCE_FEATURE_TYPE="effect"
  finalize
  jq -e '.status == "pass"' "$IMPLEMENTATION_JSON" >/dev/null
  rm -f "$GLIMMUNG_OUTPUT_FILE" "$GLIMMUNG_COMPLETION_FILE"
  emit
  jq -e '
    (.ui_hint | fromjson | .menu_label == "paper-lanterns")
    and .branch_name == "glimmung/run-1"
  ' "$GLIMMUNG_OUTPUT_FILE" >/dev/null

  # passing implementation WITHOUT a ui_hint under a standing feature type:
  # finalize fails the step, named, before any verify spend
  cat >"${IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR}/issue-agent-implementation.json" <<'JSON'
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "status": "pass",
  "summary": "added paper lanterns"
}
JSON
  set +e
  finalize
  FINALIZE_RC=$?
  set -e
  [ "$FINALIZE_RC" -ne 0 ]
  jq -e '.status == "fail" and .abort_reason == "missing_ui_hint"' "$IMPLEMENTATION_JSON" >/dev/null

  # a non-/dev route is rejected the same way
  cat >"${IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR}/issue-agent-implementation.json" <<'JSON'
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "status": "pass",
  "summary": "added paper lanterns",
  "ui_hint": {"menu_label": "paper-lanterns", "route": "https://evil.example/dev/paper-lanterns"}
}
JSON
  set +e
  finalize
  FINALIZE_RC=$?
  set -e
  [ "$FINALIZE_RC" -ne 0 ]
  jq -e '.abort_reason == "missing_ui_hint"' "$IMPLEMENTATION_JSON" >/dev/null

  # without a feature type, a missing ui_hint is not an error (generated-path
  # workflows have no standing case to bind)
  unset AMBIENCE_FEATURE_TYPE
  cat >"${IMPL_EVIDENCE_DIR}/issue-agent-implementation.json" <<'JSON'
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "status": "pass",
  "summary": "non-effect change"
}
JSON
  finalize
  jq -e '.status == "pass"' "$IMPLEMENTATION_JSON" >/dev/null

input.command:
prev=""
while true; do
  cur=$(kubectl get jobs -n glimmung-runs -o json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.items[] | select(.metadata.name | startswith("glim-ambience-167-runs-13-1")) | .metadata.name + ": " + (if (.status.conditions // [] | map(select(.type=="Complete" and .status=="True")) | length) > 0 then "Complete" elif (.status.conditions // [] | map(select(.type=="Failed" and .status=="True")) | length) > 0 then "FAILED" elif (.status.active // 0) > 0 then "Running" else "Pending" end)' | sort) || { sleep 30; continue; }
  comm -13 <(echo "$prev") <(echo "$cur") | grep -E "Complete|FAILED" || true
  prev="$cur"
  if echo "$cur" | grep -q "env-destroy-final.*\(Complete\|FAILED\)"; then
    echo "run 13.1 final cleanup terminal — run is over"
    exit 0
  fi
  sleep 30
done

payload.text:
All authorized work is landed and the fresh run is in flight on the shape we actually care about. Where things stand:

## Run 13.1 — live on the final shape
[glimmung.romaine.life/projects/ambience/issues/167/runs/13/cycles/1](https://glimmung.romaine.life/projects/ambience/issues/167/runs/13/cycles/1)

- `issue-contract`: **platform-skipped, zero pods, full resolution trace** in the graph
- `env-prep` building; next: `llm-test-plan` skips the same way, `llm-implement` runs (~30-50min), then the standing acceptance case judges the lanterns against the issue text
- Monitor armed; I'll report each phase and the verdict

## Tonight's ledger (4 repos)
| Repo | Change |
|---|---|
| glimmung | #764 `when`/`vars`/zero-compute skips + #765 dispatch round-trip hotfix — both deployed |
| ambience | #318 feature types + standing case + ui_hint, #320 issue-contract prune + declaration-over-prediction — merged |
| mcp-glimmung | #71 `vars` passthrough — deployed |
| registration | `ambience.default` → `wfs_0bf143aa…` (vars + two `when` conditions, pins intact) |

Plus two real bugs the work itself surfaced and fixed: the skipped-conclusion **failure-masking** bug in phase aggregation, and the vars round-trip drop that 422'd the first conditional dispatch.

## One open item with an operational note
**Spirelens** (task #14): its re-registration is a bigger migration — its live row uses the retired evidence-gate shape and implicit verify recycle policy, so any re-register forces the full modernization, plus its repo scripts need contract-tolerance before pruning issue-contract there. **Caveat to know now:** glimmung #764 deleted the `skip_when_preserve_test_env` runtime branch, so until spirelens re-registers with the `when` form, a spirelens run with `preserve_test_env=true` *will* tear down its env at cleanup_early. No spirelens runs are in flight; I'd take this as the next work item — say the word, or I'll flag it again after run 13.1's verdict.

If you'd rather it be deleted from the registration outright, that's a one-call re-register — say the word.
